items.title: Beijing is shifting its Taiwan strategy — here is why
items.description: It began with a dish once served to Nixon in 1971. Now Beijing is using the same stage to reshape the Taiwan question. China is advancing a slower strategy: using economic incentives and political engagement to influence Taiwan's future.

items.title: World's oldest gorilla celebrates 69th birthday at Berlin Zoo
items.description: Lady Fatou, known as the "grand dame" of the Berlin Zoo, was certified last year by Guiness as the oldest living gorilla in the world.

items.description: La Liga leaders Barcelona face Atletico Madrid, hoping to bounce back from their first-leg football defeat at home.

items.title: US-Iran talks in Pakistan end without breakthrough
items.description: Marathon negotiations between the United States and Iran in the Pakistani capital ended early Sunday without a breakthrough, with both sides failing to bridge deep differences over nuclear issues, the Strait of Hormuz, and sanctions relief, officials said.

items.title: On patrol with Canadian forces securing the Arctic as global threats grow
items.description: Across 5,200 km of Canada's Arctic, its military spent two months on a mission to meet the challenges of a changing north.

items.title: Middle East War: Iran threatens Persian Gulf, Gulf of Oman
items.description: Iran's military has warned that no ports in the Persian gulf or Gulf of Oman will be safe if its own ports are threatened. It comes as the US military announced it will launch a blockade of all Iranian ports and coastal areas.

items.title: Peru presidential election results delayed after thousands get one-day voting extension

items.description: Keiko Fujimori looked set to face a conservative rival in a runoff vote. More than 30 candidates had been contention for the presidency.

items.title: Italian PM condemns ally Trump over 'unacceptable' Pope criticism
items.description: Italy's prime minister and the US president are close allies, but Trump has refused to apologise to the "very weak" Pope Leo XIV.

items.title: At least 30 people dead following tragic stampede at Haiti fort
items.description: A tragic stampede at a mountaintop fortress in northern Haiti has left at least 30 people dead and dozens injured, after crowds gathered for traditional festivities.

France: Parliament debates bill to simplify return of stolen artworks
items.description: It still has tens of thousands of artworks and artefacts that it looted from its colonial empire. Now, France is debating a bill to simplify returning them to their countries of origin.

items.title: Hungary: jubilant scenes across Budapest follow Magyar’s victory
items.description: Hungary saw large crowds take to the streets on Sunday, 12 April 2026, after Prime Minister Viktor Orbán conceded defeat, ending 16 years in power.

items.description: Orban's exit signals potential end to Hungary's EU isolation and opens door for diplomatic and economic collaboration.

items.description: Tensions between Cuba and the U.S. remain high despite both sides acknowledging talks, although no details have been shared

items.description: The injured were shifted to a nearby hospital for treatment, officials said

items.title: Sailor records tense exchange between the US and Iran in the Strait of Hormuz
items.description: A tense exchange between the United States and Iran, recorded by a sailor in the Persian Gulf who has been speaking with the ABC, shows the extremely fraught situation in the Strait of Hormuz, as a fragile ceasefire hangs in the balance.

items.title: Algeria: Pope Leo makes first papal visit
items.description: Pope Leo touched down in Algeria on Monday, beginning an 11 day tour of four African countries. Leo is visiting Algeria to champion Christian-Muslim co-existence.

items.title: U.S., Israel met ‘strategic defeat’ in Iran, says Ambassador Fathali
items.description: The Envoy said the U.S. side made "unlawful" demands during the talks that led to the negotiations ending without results

items.title: Benin heads to a pivotal transition as votes are counted
items.description: Benin began counting ballots Sunday after a presidential election that could extend a decade of economic growth while testing the country’s democratic balance. Finance Minister Romuald Wadagni is the frontrunner to succeed outgoing president Patrice Talon, who is stepping down after two terms.

items.title: Why this disillusioned Trump voter spends hours searching Epstein files
items.description: Many who support the president have expressed frustration with how Trump's former attorney general Pam Bondi handled the Epstein files.

items.title: End of the Orban era: Hungary and Europe breathe again
items.description: Viktor Orban's Fidesz party was swept aside in Hungary's parliamentary election. His defeat is a blow for Putin, Trump and populists the world over. Election winner Peter Magyar is promising major change.

items.title: Coachella boasted big-name artists — the special guests were maybe even bigger
items.description: The world's most talked about music festival took place over the weekend, boasting a bevy of unexpected highlights.

items.title: French lawmakers to debate bill to return looted colonial-era art
items.description: France's lawmakers will on Monday debate a bill to simplify the return of artworks looted during the colonial era to their countries of origin. France still has in its possession tens of thousands of artworks and other prized artefacts that it looted from its colonial empire. FRANCE 24's Carys Garland reports.

items.title: Donald Trump 'hasn't read the psychology of Iranians well at all', military expert says
items.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, former British Military Intelligence officer Philip Ingram says that "Donald Trump hasn't read the psychology of the Iranians well at all" adding that "the Iranians are not going to give in, and therefore Donald Trump either has to continue the blockade and deal with the impact that that's going to have on global economies [...] or go back to a bombing campaign".

items.title: Judge dismisses Trump's $10 billion lawsuit against WSJ, Murdoch over reporting on ties to Epstein
items.description: The ruling marks yet another blow in the Trump administration’s efforts to manage fallout over its release of the Epstein files and the President’s attempts to use the legal system to chill reporting he finds critical of him

items.title: Morning news brief
items.description: President Trump says U.S. will blockade the Strait of Hormuz, failed U.S.-Iran negotiations cause oil prices to surge, Hungary's Viktor Orbán loses reelection bid.

items.title: Hungarians look to changed future after pro-EU Magyar's election landslide
items.description: Hungarians were waking up to a ​political earthquake on Monday, after a landslide victory for the centre-right opposition reverberated everywhere from Washington to Kyiv, sending local markets surging and turning Budapest into a party zone. Magyar has pledged a sweeping anti-corruption drive, including stronger judicial independence and tougher public procurement rules to help secure the release of the EU funds.

items.title: Trump lambasts Pope Leo XIV, extending feud over Iran war with first American pontiff
items.description: Pope Leo, who is known for choosing his words carefully, ‌has emerged as an outspoken ​critic of the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran that ⁠began on February 28

items.title: Malawi's former finance minister arrested for corruption as post-election crackdown continues
items.description: Malawian police arrested the former finance minister Sunday on suspicion of money laundering and abuse of office, in the latest move in a widening post-election crackdown on former senior officials.

items.title: Hungary's Orban defeated: France's far-right loses an ally
items.description: Congratulations from across Europe poured in Monday for Hungarian political newcomer Peter Magyar, who defeated nationalist Viktor Orban in elections seen as a blow to right-wing populism worldwide. In France, the outcome represents a setback for Marine Le Pen’s far-right party, as FRANCE 24’s French Politics Editor Marc Perelman explains.

items.title: Germany: 1 in 4 residents has 'immigration history'
items.description: Every fourth person living in Germany has either moved there from abroad or been born to parents who are both immigrants. The new statistics are a modest increase on the 2024 figures.

Music show: Papooz set sail on a journey of friendship with new album
items.description: On today’s Arts24 Music Show, Jennifer Ben Brahim speaks with Ulysse Cottin, one half of French indie duo Papooz. The band is celebrating ten years of music with a new album, "Papooz and Friends" – a sunny journey featuring some of their favourite collaborators. We also hear from British multi-hyphenate Wesley Joseph, who has just released his much-anticipated debut album, "Forever Ends Someday". The record is steeped in nostalgia, with much of it written in his childhood home of Walsall, in the West Midlands.

items.title: Electoral earthquake: Hungary vote triggers setback for Putin and Trump
items.description: Hungarian voters on Sunday ousted long-serving Prime Minister Viktor Orban after 16 years in power, rejecting the authoritarian policies and global far-right movement that he embodied in favor of a pro-European challenger in a bombshell election result with global repercussions. It was a stunning blow for Orbán — a close ally of both US President Donald Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin — who conceded defeat after what he called a ″painful″ election result.

items.title: Giant marble statues guard Italy’s seas from illegal fishing
items.description: An Italian fisherman has come up with an innovative way to protect the seabed from industrial fishing. He has created an underwater museum featuring 49 marble sculptures, designed to deter illegal trawling vessels. As a result, fish are returning to the area, and marine biodiversity is beginning to thrive once again.

items.description: As Pope Leo XIV makes a historic visit to Algeria, in the spirit of interfaith dialogue, François Picard is pleased to welcome Father Fred Wekesa, Rector of the Basilica of Saint Augustine in Annaba. He argues that interreligious dialogue must extend beyond institutions into families and daily life.

With the Lebanese and Israeli ambassadors to the United States set ​to meet on Tuesday ‌in Washington, Lebanon's foreign minister said Beirut would use face-to-face negotiations to press for a ceasefire in the war, which has complicated ⁠wider diplomacy to halt the conflict in the Middle East.

items.title: Trump's defamation lawsuit against Wall Street Journal over Epstein story dismissed
items.description: The case related to a Wall Street Journal article that described a sexually suggestive letter that the newspaper said was included in a 2003 album compiled for Epstein’s 50th birthday.

items.title: First gay rights movement: Berlin's wild 1920s queer history
items.description: Before the Nazis took power, Berlin was a center of LGBTQ+ rights and queer culture, with research institutes, a flourishing nightlife and one of the earliest gay rights' movements.

items.title: South Korea, Poland to upgrade ties as Donald Tusk calls Seoul key ally after U.S.
items.description: South ⁠Korean President Lee said the countries would further expand defence ⁠industry cooperation under a $44.2 billion framework pact signed in 2022

items.title: At least 30 feared dead in crush at Haitian tourist site
items.description: The incident occurred at a "tourist event attended by many young people", Haiti's prime minister says.

items.title: News24 | First woman to lead Australian army a ‘historic moment’ amid sex allegations fallout
items.description: Australia on Monday said a woman would lead its army for the first time in history, as part of a reshuffle of the country’s defence force leadership.

items.title: News24 | Fragile Iran truce brings brief relief to African markets
items.description: The truce between the US, Israel and Iran has eased pressure on African markets. But economists warn that deep‑rooted vulnerabilities mean the rebound may be short‑lived.

items.title: Orban was defeated in Hungary, but Orbanism lives on
items.description: Viktor Orban’s stepping stone – the EU’s own illiberal tendencies and militarism - is still very much present.

items.title: Israelis war-weary but most oppose Iran ceasefire, poll suggests
items.description: The vast majority did not believe Hezbollah or Iran had been severely weakened by weeks of Israeli and US attacks, according to the poll.

items.title: Argentina’s inequality eases slightly, but income gap remains stark
items.description: Income inequality improved slightly towards end of 2025; INDEC data shows richest 10% still earn 13 times more than poorest, with sharp disparities in gender and informal employment persisting. Leer más

items.title: Atlantic Music Expo: Cape Verde celebrates its musical heritage
items.description: At the annual festival, the archipelago's brightest musical talents not only showcase their art, but also perform an essential service to Cape Verde's culture and identity by keeping traditional music alive.

items.title: News24 | Oil surges, rand slips as US-Iran peace talks collapse
items.description: Oil prices ‌surged on Monday as the US moved to impose a blockade on Iranian ⁠shipping after the collapse of weekend peace talks, while the dollar rose and stocks and bonds fell.

items.title: Australia appoints Lt. Gen. Susan Coyle as first woman to lead its Army
items.description: Lt. Gen. Susan Coyle, ​the current chief of joint capabilities, will become ⁠Chief of Army in July, the Australian government said

items.title: Slovakia's Fico ready to cooperate with Hungary's new Prime Minister
items.description: Under Fico Slovakia has been ‌a close ally of Hungary, with both ​neighbours maintaining warm ties with Moscow, opposing European Union sanctions and keeping up purchases of ⁠Russian oil and gas

items.title: Global markets slide as Donald Trump orders Strait of Hormuz blockade
items.description: Global markets have been rattled after Donald Trump announced a US naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, escalating tensions following failed peace talks with Iran.

items.title: Ex-Nigerian oil minister denies taking bribes
items.description: Diezani Alison-Madueke is accused of living “a life of luxury” in the UK at properties paid for and refurbished by industry figures.

items.title: TSMC likely to book fourth straight quarter of record profit on insatiable AI demand
Iran's new Supreme Leader is reportedly recovering from severe wounds
items.description: The news agency Reuters said on Sunday that Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei was recovering from severe and disfiguring wounds from the airstrike that killed his father, Ali Khamenei. Iran's leader has not been seen in public since his appointment.

items.title: 'We need real peace': Ukrainians have little faith in Easter truce
items.description: Easter festivities are muted in Kharkiv as Ukrainians expect fighting to flare up again after a weekend truce.

items.title: News24 | Republicans block bid to rein in Trump’s war powers, Democrats vow to force a vote
items.description: US House of Representatives Republicans blocked a Democratic effort to end US attacks on Iran, as Donald Trump’s party continued to prevent efforts to rein in the Republican president’s war powers.

items.title: In Bangladesh, bus drivers taking enormous risks to go fast and furious
items.description: In Bangladesh, a dangerous trend on roads is taking the country by storm — bus drivers taking enormous risks to go fast and furious. Buses are one of the most popular modes of transport in the country, but they face fierce competition. To attract customers, companies are promising to reduce travel times, even if it means taking all sorts of risks.

items.title: Artemis II space mission crew safely returns to Earth
items.description: An elated NASA late Friday was celebrating its successful voyage around the Moon, after four astronauts safely returned to Earth having completed the first lunar flyby in more than 50 years.

items.title: Pakistan eyes narrow window to resuscitate US-Iran talks after breakdown
items.description: US and Iran end marathon Islamabad talks without agreement, leaving fragile ceasefire under growing strain.

items.title: Nissan management shake-up plans reported as sharks circle
items.description: Nissan is considering a change in leadership following poor results and the failure of a proposed merger with Honda, according to a report citing unnamed sources.

items.title: Libya to have first unified state budget in 13 years
items.description: Libya's rival eastern and western legislative bodies have signed a US-mediated agreement to unify public spending across the divided country for the first time in over a decade, the central bank said Saturday.

items.title: InterCement deal ‘once in a lifetime opportunity,’ Mindlin says
items.description: Argentine energy mogul Marcelo Mindlin is leading a group of investors and creditors seeking to restructure Brazil’s InterCement, the parent company of Loma Negra SA. Leer más

items.title: Hungarian election: EU leaders celebrate Peter Magyar's victory
items.description: European leaders heaped praise on Peter Magyar after his stunning election victory in Hungary, not just for what the country's next premier might do but for who he is not — long-serving Prime Minister Viktor Orbán, who many saw as a direct threat to the continent's peace and prosperity. The outpouring reflected a deep frustration with Orban across the 27-nation European Union and its institutions, as FRANCE 24's Dave Keating explains, reporting from Brussels.

items.title: Peru frontrunner Keiko Fujimori vows to expel migrants, further Latin America's rightward tilt
items.description: Keiko Fujimori, 50-year-old daughter of late ex-president Alberto Fujimori, is one of 35 candidates running to be Peru's next leader in Sunday's election. Leer más

items.title: Germany: Betting on elections is illegal — but possible
items.description: Online gambling is gaining traction in Germany. Using prediction markets like Polymarket to bet on political events remains illegal, but it is almost impossible to police.

items.title: Milei pleads for patience as growth slows and approval falls
items.description: “We know that the last months were hard. That’s why we’re asking for patience. This is the right path," says President Milei in social media post. Leer más

items.title: Pope Leo brushes off Trump criticism amid growing Vatican–U.S. tensions over Iran war
items.description: Pope Leo XIV says he will not be deterred by criticism from President Trump, vowing to continue his calls for peace as tensions escalate between the Vatican and Washington over the Iran conflict.

items.title: Benin's opposition candidate concedes defeat to Wadagni in presidential vote
items.description: Benin opposition candidate Paul Hounkpe suffered a bruising election loss on Monday after conceding to favourite Romuald Wadagni in Benin's presidential vote.

items.title: Milei’s auction favours group with past China ties, US dredger warns
items.description: US dredging company calls out bid terms of President Javier Milei’s signature concession in Argentina, claiming they’re tilted in favour of a consortium with past ties to China. Leer más

items.title: News24 | Deadline passes for US blockade of Hormuz, Iran threatens retaliation against Gulf states
items.description: The deadline passed on Monday for the start of a US military blockade of ships leaving Iran’s ports, and Tehran threatened to ‌retaliate against ports of its Gulf neighbours after weekend talks on ending the war broke down.

items.title: Nigerian Air Forces launches investigation into deadly airstrike in Yobe state
items.description: Nigeria's Air Force has launched an investigation after an airstrike on Sunday allegedly killed dozens of civilians in the country's northeast.

items.title: Explainer: Milei’s attempt to change Argentina’s glacier protection law
items.description: Argentina’s Glacier Law reform push is a battle between mining ambitions and environmental protection; Milei’s proposed changes could reshape glacier safeguards, expand mining activity, and shift power to provincial governments. Leer más

items.title: Lafarge Trial: Cement firm fined, former CEO jailed
items.description: A French court has fined cement group Lafarge $1.3 million for paying the Islamic State group and other jihadists to keep its factory running in Syria during the civil war.

items.title: Celebrities and fans pay tributes to Asha Bhosle
items.description: Asha Bhosle died in Mumbai on Sunday after being admitted to the hospital following a heart attack.

items.title: News24 | Iran executed at least 1 639 people in 2025, as authorities ‘prolong their crumbling rule’
items.description: Iranian authorities executed at least 1 639 people in 2025, the highest number since 1989, two NGOs said on Monday, warning it risked using capital punishment even more extensively after protests in January and the war against Israel and the US.

items.title: Court hears teacher allegedly struck student in the face, breaking her teeth
items.description: A former Darwin teacher has pleaded not guilty to causing serious harm after allegedly striking a female student in the face and causing her to break her front teeth.

items.title: Iran war: Talks collapse is bad news for ordinary Iranians
items.description: The collapse of the US-Iran talks has cast fresh doubt over whether the two sides can bridge the gap dividing them. But whether diplomacy will succeed also depends on a range of challenges inside Iran.

items.title: Fiji Police are circling an Australian wanted over four-tonne meth import
items.description: Key insiders from Fiji's largest-ever meth bust say the drug import had alarming links to organised crime in Australia.

items.title: Peru election extended for second day after ballot delivery failure
items.description: Peruvians headed back to the polls Monday after ballot shortages forced an unprecedented extension of Sunday’s election, reflecting deep strains in a country crippled by political instability and escalating crime. Peru police on Monday said they arrested an election official amid an enquiry into the voting delays.

items.title: Opposition candidate concedes defeat in Benin presidential election
items.description: Benin presidential election favourite Romuald Wadagni headed for victory Monday as his opponent conceded defeat even as votes were still being counted.

items.title: Toyota eyes lunar rover powered by regenerative fuel-cell tech
items.description: Toyota has teamed up with JAXA since 2019 to develop the manned lunar rover — which it dubbed the Lunar Cruiser — that they hope can be put on the moon in 2029.

items.title: News24 | Record turnout Hungarian election could end Orban’s 16-year rule as pro-EU challenger leads
items.description: Hungarians flocked to vote on Sunday in a closely watched parliamentary election that could end nationalist Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s 16-year stint in power as a self-described “thorn” in the EU’s side.

items.title: Hungary: Scandal, fear and hope ahead of vote
items.description: Fake news and a growing number of political scandals: Hungary hasn't seen an election this fraught since after 1989's collapse of the communist dictatorship.

items.title: Fewer buses on Argentina's streets as fuel costs rise and subsidies go unpaid
items.description: Firms reduce number buses in circulation by between 20% and 40% as fuel prices surge amid Middle East crisis. Leer más

items.title: Trump says Iranian ships to be ‘eliminated’ as US naval blockade begins
items.description: Iran describes the siege against its ports as the US president's 'revenge of choice' against the global economy.

items.title: News24 | Tankers steering clear of Hormuz ahead of a US blockade, oil prices spike
items.description: Oil tankers are steering clear of the Strait of Hormuz ahead of a US blockade following failed peace talks ‌between the US and Iran over the weekend, shipping data showed.

items.title: Haiti: at least 30 dead in stampede at UNESCO‑listed Citadelle Laferrière fortress
items.description: At least 30 people died on Saturday, 11 April 2026, after a stampede at the Citadelle Laferrière near Milot, a major tourist site in the north of Haiti.

items.title: Lufthansa cabin crew begin strike
items.description: Hundreds of flights have been canceled as cabin crew members walk out. This is Lufthansa's third major strike this year.
